title: quercus × albescens
intro: Quercus × albescens, a hybrid of the white oak (Quercus alba) and the English oak (Quercus robur), is a deciduous tree native to North America. The bark of Quercus × albescens is used in herbal medicine due to its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus × albescens include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Quercus × albescens can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
